title: A topographic depression on the subducting Nazca plate controls the April 1st 2014 M8.1 Iquique earthquake rupture in Northern Chile
abstract: Along the Northern Chilean active continental margin, the subducting Nazca plate is characterized by a rough sea floor topography that has been suggested to control the rupture behaviour of megathrust earthquakes. The research team examined structural controls on the 2014 Iquique earthquake (M8.1), which ruptured only a portion of a seismic gap previously active in 1877. Through reprocessed CINCA95 multichannel seismic data, a topographic depression on the subducting Nazca plate is imaged and linked to controlling the earthquake rupture. Geodetic and seismological analysis combined with seismic structural observations supports the role of seafloor morphology in controlling the Iquique rupture.

location.description: Offshore northern Chile between 19°S and 21°S, 2014 Iquique earthquake rupture zone and subducting Nazca plate topography

data.name: CINCA95 Multichannel Seismic Reflection Data (reprocessed, ~19°–21°S)
data.description: CINCA95 MCS reflection data from 1995 reprocessed with PSDM to image a topographic depression on the subducting Nazca plate that controlled the 2014 Iquique M8.1 earthquake rupture.

processing.summary: Same CINCA95 dataset (1995) as used in Storch et al. (2021) reprocessed with pre-stack depth migration to image the northern Chilean subduction zone at 19°–21°S. This 2023 follow-up paper focuses on imaging a specific topographic depression on the subducting Nazca plate and its correlation with the 2014 Iquique earthquake rupture pattern. Combined with geodetic (GPS) and seismological analysis.
workflow: 6. Integration with 2014 Iquique earthquake hypocentres and aftershocks
processing.notes: Companion paper to Storch et al. (2021, GJI). Same CINCA95 dataset reprocessed with updated PSDM workflow. Behind paywall in Tectonophysics.

analysis_notes: Paper behind paywall; no open access version found. Abstract reconstructed from survey snippet. Continuation of Storch et al. (2021) work using same CINCA95 (1995) dataset. GFZ Potsdam authors (Storch, Buske, Victor, Oncken). CINCA95 data held at GEOMAR/GFZ.
